title: daphne odora subsp. acutiloba
intro: Daphne odora subsp. acutiloba, also known as the fragrant winter daphne or Japanese daphne, is a perennial shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its dense, compact growth habit and clusters of small, star-shaped flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Daphne odora subsp. acutiloba include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically harvested and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions.
